text.title: Gcp Networking Vpc Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-networking-vpc methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Conflicts with hybrid topologies; wastes IP space across all regions.
antipattern.fix: Recreate VPC in custom mode with explicit per-region subnets.

antipattern.symptom: Firewall rules drift; identity not bound to tag.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to service-account-based firewall rules where available.

antipattern.symptom: Same rule maintained per-VPC; auditability poor.
antipattern.fix: Consolidate via hierarchical firewall policies at org/folder level.

antipattern.symptom: Cluster recreation required to add IP-alias secondary ranges.
antipattern.root-cause: Subnet hosting GKE has no secondary ranges.
antipattern.fix: Recreate subnet with secondary ranges for pods + services BEFORE cluster create.
